name: MCP Azure Server
description: Official Microsoft MCP server for Azure cloud services, generally available as Azure MCP Server 2.0 (2026-04-10) with tools spanning 44+ Azure service areas including compute, storage, databases, AI/ML, monitoring, and governance. Development now lives in the microsoft/mcp monorepo (the former Azure/azure-mcp repo is archived). Installable via npm (@azure/mcp), MCPB bundles for Claude Desktop, VS Code, NuGet, PyPI, and Docker, with a self-hosted remote HTTP mode.

evidence.value: Built on Azure's reliable REST APIs with 99.9% SLA
High success rate for resource management operations
Respects Azure throttling limits with retry logic
Performance varies by region; generally good latency
Handles Azure API errors with retry and fallback logic
Credentials handled through the official Azure Identity SDK: Azure CLI (az login), environment credentials, managed identity, and Microsoft Entra ID; no raw secrets in MCP client config required

authentication_security.methodology: Authentication mechanism review; small raise from 82 reflecting standardized Azure Identity SDK credential chain in the GA release

evidence.value: Azure credentials stored locally; AI can perform actions within scope
Respects Azure RBAC permissions but requires careful role assignment
AI can delete resources, modify configurations, and manage infrastructure
All Azure operations logged in Activity Log
Can modify network security groups and firewall rules
Azure resource metadata and configurations sent to LLM provider
May expose connection strings, keys, and secrets in resource configurations
Respects Azure policy and compliance settings but data leaves Azure
Azure resource data shared with LLM provider per their privacy policy
Azure data residency requirements may conflict with LLM data sharing
Comprehensive documentation with Azure-specific examples
All operations logged in Azure Activity Log and MCP logs
Open source implementation from Microsoft
GA documentation enumerates supported service areas and tools with per-service documentation in the microsoft/mcp monorepo

api_coverage_clarity.methodology: API documentation review; raised from 70 as GA docs now enumerate the tool surface

evidence.value: Installable via npm (@azure/mcp), drag-and-drop MCPB bundles for Claude Desktop (no runtime required), VS Code integration, NuGet, PyPI, and Docker; authenticates with existing az login session

ease_of_setup.methodology: Setup complexity assessment; raised from 75 due to MCPB bundles and az-login-based auth removing service principal setup for interactive use

evidence.value: Performance depends on Azure service and region (typically 100-800ms)
Depends on Azure service SLAs (typically 99.9%+)
2.0 GA covers 44+ Azure service areas across compute, storage, databases, AI/ML, monitoring, and governance

service_coverage.methodology: Feature coverage assessment; raised from 78 as GA 2.0 substantially expanded service coverage

evidence.value: Active first-party development in the microsoft/mcp monorepo with frequent releases; growing adoption via VS Code, Claude Desktop MCPB bundles, and Visual Studio integration

code-generation.notes: Excellent for infrastructure-as-code generation and Azure automation

customer-support.notes: Useful for Azure infrastructure troubleshooting and support

content-creation.notes: Limited applicability; mainly for infrastructure documentation

data-analysis.notes: Good for analyzing Azure resource metrics and cost data

research-assistant.notes: Useful for researching Azure configurations and best practices

legal-compliance.notes: Risk of exposing infrastructure details; careful access control needed

healthcare.notes: Risk of exposing HIPAA-regulated infrastructure; requires careful controls

financial-analysis.notes: Good for Azure cost analysis but risk of exposing financial infrastructure

education.notes: Excellent for teaching Azure, cloud infrastructure, and DevOps

creative-writing.notes: Low relevance to creative writing workflows

best_for: Azure DevOps engineers automating cloud infrastructure management
Cloud architects working with Azure resources and cost optimization
Educators teaching Azure and cloud computing concepts
strengths: Official Microsoft implementation, generally available as 2.0 since April 2026
Broad Azure resource management: tools across 44+ Azure service areas
Built on reliable Azure APIs with strong SLAs
Full operation auditability through Azure Activity Log
Open source (MIT) in the microsoft/mcp monorepo with Microsoft support
Supports Azure RBAC for granular permissions; credentials via the official Azure Identity SDK
Flexible distribution: npm, MCPB bundles, VS Code/Visual Studio integration, NuGet, PyPI, Docker, self-hosted remote HTTP
limitations: Azure resource metadata and configurations exposed to LLM provider
Risk of destructive operations (resource deletion, network changes); Microsoft warns autonomous or misconfigured clients may perform destructive actions and recommends least-privilege roles
May expose connection strings, keys, and secrets in resource configurations
Agent inherits the full RBAC permissions of the authenticated identity
Not every Azure service is covered despite broad 2.0 GA coverage
Data residency considerations with LLM provider data sharing

supported_platforms: Windows, macOS, Linux (x64/ARM64) via npm, MCPB bundles, NuGet, PyPI, Docker
metadata.api_dependency: Azure REST API, Azure SDK, Azure Identity SDK
metadata.authentication: Microsoft Entra ID via Azure Identity SDK (az login, environment credentials, managed identity)
